tasks.subject: [memory-recall-indicator] [P1] Add MemoryRecallPartSchema and extend MessagePart union
tasks.description:
Extend the client-rendered MessagePart discriminated union in `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts` with a new `memory_recall` variant, and re-export the inferred type from `packages/shared/src/types.ts`.

## Files to modify

- `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/types.ts`

## Implementation

1. Add `MemoryRecallPartSchema` immediately after `ElicitationPartSchema` (around line 886), before the `MessagePartSchema` discriminated union declaration (line 888). Copy this exact schema:

```ts
export const MemoryRecallPartSchema = z
  .object({
    type: z.literal('memory_recall'),
    mode: z.enum(['select', 'synthesize']),
    memories: z.array(
      z.object({
        /** Absolute path to the memory file, or `<synthesis:DIR>` sentinel when mode is 'synthesize'. */
        path: z.string(),
        scope: z.enum(['personal', 'team']),
        /** Synthesis paragraph. Only present when mode is 'synthesize'. */
        content: z.string().optional(),
      })
    ),
    isStreaming: z.boolean().optional(),
  })
  .openapi('MemoryRecallPart');

export type MemoryRecallPart = z.infer<typeof MemoryRecallPartSchema>;
```

2. Extend the existing `MessagePartSchema` discriminated union at `schemas.ts:888`:

```ts
export const MessagePartSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('type', [
  TextPartSchema,
  ToolCallPartSchema,
  BackgroundTaskPartSchema,
  ThinkingPartSchema,
  ErrorPartSchema,
  ElicitationPartSchema,
  MemoryRecallPartSchema, // new
]);
```

3. Re-export `MemoryRecallPart` from `packages/shared/src/types.ts` alongside the existing `ThinkingPart`, `BackgroundTaskPart`, etc. Insert it in the sorted `export type { … }` block (there's an existing `ThinkingPart` export around line 82):

```ts
export type {
  // ...
  ThinkingPart,
  MemoryRecallPart,
  // ...
};
```

## Design notes

- **`isStreaming?: boolean`** mirrors `ThinkingPartSchema` exactly — this lets `MemoryRecallBlock` reuse the `ThinkingBlock` auto-collapse useEffect verbatim.
- This is a **union extension**, not a breaking change. Existing runtime validators that encounter `memory_recall` before the schema is updated will reject the part; since the client is the only consumer of `MessagePart` and this spec ships the client changes atomically, there's no cross-version risk.
- Do NOT modify the existing `MemoryRecallEventSchema` (the wire event) at `schemas.ts:598-613`. It's the server-emitted event; this task adds the **client-side rendered state** counterpart.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes across the monorepo.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (no new TSDoc warnings on exported schema/type).
- [ ] `MemoryRecallPartSchema.parse({ type: 'memory_recall', mode: 'select', memories: [{ path: '~/.claude/CLAUDE.md', scope: 'personal' }] })` does not throw.
- [ ] `MemoryRecallPartSchema.parse({ type: 'memory_recall', mode: 'synthesize', memories: [{ path: '<synthesis:~/.claude>', scope: 'team', content: 'A summary…' }], isStreaming: true })` does not throw.
- [ ] `MemoryRecallPartSchema.parse({ type: 'memory_recall' })` throws (missing required fields).
- [ ] `MessagePartSchema` accepts a `memory_recall` part alongside existing types without altering their schemas.
- [ ] `import type { MemoryRecallPart } from '@dorkos/shared/types'` resolves in downstream apps.
tasks.activeForm: Adding MemoryRecallPartSchema and extending MessagePart union

tasks.subject: [memory-recall-indicator] [P3] Extend CollapsibleCard with 'memory' variant
tasks.description:
The existing `CollapsibleCard` primitive at `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/primitives/CollapsibleCard.tsx` currently supports `variant?: 'default' | 'thinking'`. Extend it to accept `'memory'` as a third variant so `MemoryRecallBlock` can pass `variant="memory"` per §9.4 of the spec.

## File to modify

- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/primitives/CollapsibleCard.tsx`

## Changes

1. Update the `variant` prop type:

```ts
/** Visual variant: default for tool calls, thinking for reasoning blocks, memory for recall blocks. */
variant?: 'default' | 'thinking' | 'memory';
```

2. Add a matching border-left treatment in the `cn()` call (current values: `border-l-muted-foreground/30` for default, `border-l-muted-foreground/20` for thinking). Use `border-l-muted-foreground/20` for memory (matches thinking — both are secondary signals):

```tsx
variant === 'memory' && 'border-l-muted-foreground/20',
```

3. Update the TSDoc comment on the component if needed to mention the new variant.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] Existing `CollapsibleCard` tests at `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/primitives/__tests__/CollapsibleCard.test.tsx` still pass (`pnpm vitest run <path>`).
- [ ] Passing `variant="memory"` renders without console errors.

tasks.subject: [memory-recall-indicator] [P5] Accessibility, reduced-motion, and docs polish
tasks.description:
Final polish pass covering ARIA, keyboard flow, reduced-motion, and docs updates per §12, §10.6, and §14 of the spec.

## Polish checklist

### Accessibility (§10.6, §12)

- [ ] Chip button has `aria-expanded` and `aria-label={headerLabel}` (inherited from `CollapsibleCard`). Verify manually.
- [ ] Row buttons have accessible names: `aria-label={`Copy path ${path}`}` for `select`, `aria-label="Copy synthesized memory content"` for `synthesize`.
- [ ] Scope icons carry `aria-label="Personal memory"` / `"Team memory"` — not decorative.
- [ ] Focus-visible outlines use `focus-ring` utility per `.claude/rules/components.md`.
- [ ] Keyboard flow: Tab to chip → Enter/Space to expand → Tab through rows → Enter/Space to copy. Verify no focus traps.

### Reduced-motion (§12)

- [ ] Confirm `animate-tasks` (used on the streaming label + icon) respects `prefers-reduced-motion`. If it doesn't, wrap in `motion-safe:animate-tasks` or add a media-query guard in the relevant CSS. Reuse whatever pattern `ThinkingBlock` already uses — do not diverge.

### Mobile (§10.5)

- [ ] Collapsed chip fits on one line at 320px. No hover-only affordances. (Covered by tests in task 3.4.)
- [ ] Tap targets ≥ 44px tall. (Covered by tests in task 3.4.)

### Performance (§13)

- [ ] Verify `BookOpen`, `Sparkles`, `User`, `Users` icon imports don't duplicate imports already present elsewhere (grep the codebase). Tree-shaking should dedupe, but consolidate imports if any are in an icon-barrel file.
- [ ] No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. Paths and synthesis content render as plain React children.

### Docs (§14)

- [ ] Add a short entry in `contributing/state-management.md` (or `contributing/data-fetching.md` — pick whichever discusses stream-event parts) describing the new `memory_recall` part and its lifecycle, pointing at `MemoryRecallBlock.tsx` as the canonical example alongside `ThinkingBlock.tsx`.
- [ ] TSDoc present on `MemoryRecallPartSchema`, `MemoryRecallPart`, `MemoryRecallBlock`, `upsertMemoryRecallPart`, `truncateMiddle` per `.claude/rules/documentation.md`.
- [ ] Changelog: add a single-line entry to the pending release notes: `Chat: Memory recall indicator — see which memory files shaped each response.` (Use `writing-changelogs` skill if available.)
- [ ] No `apps/site/` user docs update required for this release.

### Playground (optional but encouraged per `maintaining-dev-playground`)

- [ ] Add a `MemoryRecallBlock` showcase to `apps/client/src/dev/` so designers/reviewers can see all 4 states (streaming select, streaming synthesize, collapsed, expanded with many paths).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Manual keyboard run-through confirms full interaction is possible without mouse.
- [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` disables the breathing animation (verify in browser devtools).
- [ ] TSDoc coverage is complete on exported symbols.
- [ ] `contributing/` guide entry added.
- [ ] Changelog entry drafted.
- [ ] `pnpm test && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint` all green across the monorepo.
tasks.activeForm: Finalizing accessibility, reduced-motion, and docs polish
